Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Airtable for all relocation records assigned to Suki Patel using airtable_search_records (field: "Assigned Coordinator", value: "Suki Patel"). Discovery: Multiple "In Progress" records whose move end dates have already passed — Deshawn Hartley, Jae-won Kim, Simone Richter, Marcus Webb, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya, Daniel Reeves, Maya Johansson, Andre Mitchell (Mosaic Robotics, Columbus OH → Boise ID, April 22–24). Jae-won Kim's record has a note about an unsigned lease and landlord holdout on guarantor paperwork.
OE 2: Search emails for the StormCloud / Jae-won Kim lease situation using search_emails (query: "Jae-won Kim" or "guarantor" or "Carmen Reyes"). Discovery: Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest emailed Suki on April 8 about the unsigned Barton Creek Flats lease needing a corporate guarantor packet. Suki replied April 9 saying she would handle it but never followed through. Lease remains unsigned; Jae-won is still in temporary housing. Escalated by StormCloud CFO Liam Park on April 24.
OE 3: Search Slack for internal flags on Suki's moves using conversations_search_messages (query: "Suki" or "Jae-won" or "StormCloud"). Discovery: Chloe's ops audit documenting the Jae-won guarantor failure chain, Suki's own admission of the miss, and Mina's StormCloud incident reconstruction discussion. Also find BrightLoop issues flagged internally.
OE 4: Search Linear for existing tickets related to Suki's relocations using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud" or "BrightLoop"). Discovery: Jae-won Kim relocation ticket closed prematurely (assigned to Suki), StormCloud reconstruction ticket (P1, assigned to Mina), BrightLoop audit ticket (P1, assigned to Mina), BrightLoop ops gaps ticket (P2, assigned to Chloe). No dedicated tickets for Deshawn Hartley, Terrence Nakamura, or Camille Oduya moves.
OE 5: Search emails for BrightLoop issues using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or "Marcus Webb" or "Road Runner"). Discovery: Simone Richter was placed in a studio instead of promised 1BR — complaint went unanswered ~8 days before Julian and Mina picked it up. Road Runner sent April 11 delay notice about Marcus Webb's Honda Civic stuck in Indianapolis — Blessing missed it, Marcus sent two unanswered follow-ups, Julian stepped in.
OE 6: Search emails for Vectral relocation details using search_emails (query: "Terrence Nakamura" or "Camille Oduya"). Discovery: Terrence requires ADA-accessible housing (wheelchair user, roll-in shower, 36-inch doorways, split shipment). Camille has a hard April 12 pickup with apartment pre-secured by Vectral. Mina is AM; Suki is coordinator for both.
OE 7: Search emails for Deshawn Hartley / Mosaic status using search_emails (query: "Deshawn Hartley" or "Automate 2026"). Discovery: Hard April 19 deadline for Automate 2026 trade show. Swift Relocations is the vendor, Suki is coordinator, Emeka is AM. Move end date has passed — Airtable still shows "In Progress."
OE 8: Look up contact details for account managers and Chloe using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina Hashimoto", "Emeka Diallo", "Chloe Vance"). Discovery: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, emeka.diallo@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com.
OE 9: Update Airtable relocation records using airtable_update_records to reflect actual status — mark completed moves (Deshawn Hartley, Daniel Reeves, Maya Johansson, Camille Oduya, Terrence Nakamura, Andre Mitchell) as "Completed," add notes on records with known issues (Simone Richter apartment mismatch, Marcus Webb vehicle delay, Jae-won Kim unresolved lease and guarantor packet never sent to UrbanNest).
OE 10: Create Linear ticket(s) using linear_create_issue for moves without existing tracking tickets — Deshawn Hartley completion closeout, Vectral ADA/tight-timeline moves. Add comments to existing tickets using linear_create_comment (StormCloud reconstruction, BrightLoop audit) rather than creating duplicates.
OE 11: Post summary in Slack #operations (channel_id: C006) using conversations_add_message — covering status of all Suki's relocations, which records were updated, known open issues, and flagging workload for Chloe's reassignment consideration.
OE 12: Send emails to account managers using send_email (from: suki.patel@moveops.com). Email Mina Hashimoto about BrightLoop/Vectral/StormCloud status from coordinator perspective. Email Emeka Diallo about Mosaic/Deshawn Hartley completion status. Flag any items requiring AM follow-up with clients.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 At least one Airtable relocation record was updated (via `airtable_update_records`) for a relocation assigned to Suki Patel.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓—✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called `airtable_update_records` twice targeting records in tblRelocations01 — first batch updating 7 records (Daniel Reeves, Maya Johansson, Camille Oduya, Terrence Nakamura, Deshawn Hartley, Andre Mitchell, Simone Richter), second batch updating 2 records (Marcus Webb and Jae-won Kim) with corrective notes. All are assigned to Suki Patel.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows a clear `airtable_update_records` tool call targeting base `appMoveOpsOps001`, table `tblRelocations01`, with 8 records updated — all assigned to Suki Patel.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows two `airtable_update_records` calls targeting `tblRelocations01` in base `appMoveOpsOps001`, covering 9 relocation records assigned to Suki Patel, including Jae-won Kim, Terrence Nakamura, Simone Richter, Marcus Webb, and others.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows two `airtable_update_records` calls updating multiple records assigned to Suki Patel (Deshawn Hartley, Daniel Reeves, Maya Johansson, Andre Mitchell, Simone Richter, Marcus Webb, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya) in the tblRelocations01 table.
—Run #5No justification
✓Run #6The trajectory shows a `mcp__mcp_de9c927b__airtable_update_records` call targeting base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001', table_id 'tblRelocations01' with 9 records updated, all assigned to Suki Patel.
2 The Airtable update changes the status of at least one of the following overdue relocations from "In Progress" to "Completed": Deshawn Hartley, Daniel Reeves, Maya Johansson, Camille Oduya, Terrence Nakamura, or Andre Mitchell, OR adds corrective notes to at least one record with a known issue (Jae-won Kim, Simone Richter, or Marcus Webb).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓—✓ 100%

✓Run #2The `airtable_update_records` call adds detailed status review notes to multiple records including overdue moves (Deshawn Hartley, Camille Oduya, Terrence Nakamura, etc.) and known issue records (Simone Richter studio mismatch, Marcus Webb vehicle delay, Jae-won Kim unsigned lease). Both overdue records and issue records were updated with corrective notes.
✓Run #3The Airtable updates added detailed STATUS NOTE fields to multiple records with known issues: Jae-won Kim (unsigned lease), Simone Richter (studio vs. 1BR), Marcus Webb (vehicle shipping status), Terrence Nakamura (ADA housing unconfirmed), Camille Oduya (stipend unverified), and others. This clearly constitutes adding corrective notes to records with known issues.
✓Run #4The Airtable updates add detailed corrective 'STATUS REVIEW' notes to multiple records with known issues: Marcus Webb (vehicle shipping blocker flagged), Terrence Nakamura (ADA/housing gaps flagged), Camille Oduya (stipend gap flagged), and Simone Richter (temp housing verification needed). The Jae-won Kim record was also corrected from 'Completed' to 'In Progress' as noted in the #operations message.
—Run #5No justification
✓Run #6The airtable_update_records call changes status to 'Completed' for Deshawn Hartley, Andre Mitchell (recReloc00000000038), Daniel Reeves (recReloc00000000031), Maya Johansson (recReloc00000000032), and adds detailed corrective issue notes to Jae-won Kim, Simone Richter, and Marcus Webb records.
3 At least one Linear issue was created (via `linear_create_issue`) or a comment was added to an existing Linear issue (via `linear_create_comment`) related to one of Suki's relocations.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓—✓ 100%

✓Run #2The trajectory shows four `linear_create_issue` calls: Vectral batch close-out (Daniel Reeves & Maya Johansson), Terrence Nakamura ADA/split shipment (P1), Camille Oduya stipend/move close-out, and Mosaic batch close-out (Deshawn Hartley & Andre Mitchell). Additionally, `linear_list_issues` was used to check existing tickets before creating new ones.
✓Run #3Six `linear_create_issue` calls were made (OPS-483 through OPS-488) covering Jae-won Kim (StormCloud), Terrence Nakamura (Vectral), Simone Richter (BrightLoop), Vectral batch, Andre Mitchell (Mosaic), and Marcus Webb (BrightLoop).
✓Run #4Four `linear_create_issue` calls are present in the trajectory: tickets for Terrence Nakamura (ADA/split shipment), Marcus Webb (vehicle shipping), Camille Oduya (stipend), and a batch verification ticket for 5 overdue moves.

✓Run #6The trajectory shows four `linear_create_issue` calls creating tickets for Terrence Nakamura (#483), Camille Oduya (#484), Marcus Webb (#485), and Simone Richter (#486). Linear read tools (linear_list_issues) were also used beforehand to check existing tickets.
4 A message was posted to the #operations Slack channel (via `conversations_add_message`, channel_id: C006).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓—✓ 100%

5 The #operations message includes: (a) a status overview of Suki's relocations, and (b) a flag about Suki's workload or capacity for Chloe's consideration regarding reassignment.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓—✓ 100%
✓Run #1The #operations message explicitly addresses Chloe, provides a full status breakdown of all Suki's relocations (critical items, completed items, open gaps), and explicitly states 'If you need to shift anything off my plate, I'd rather you know now' — serving both the status overview and workload/capacity signal functions.
✓Run #2The #operations message provides: (a) a detailed status overview of all 9 of Suki's relocations broken down by criticality, and (b) an explicit flag for Chloe — 'If anything needs to be shifted off Suki's plate, now is the time' — with cc @chloe.vance.
✓Run #3The #operations message includes a detailed status overview for all 8 relocations (sections for Critical, High Priority, and Likely Complete) and explicitly tells Chloe: 'If you need to redistribute any of the open items, the data is current as of right now,' flagging Suki's capacity situation and enabling reassignment decisions.
✓Run #4The #operations message (channel_id C006) includes a full red/yellow/green status breakdown of all relocations and explicitly states 'Chloe, if you need to shift anything off my plate, please do. I'd rather you know now than have a client find out first.' Both requirements are met.
—Run #5No justification
✓Run #6The #operations message includes a full status overview of all 11 relocations (3 critical, 2 needing verification, 6 completed) and explicitly flags workload capacity: 'If you need to shift anything off my plate to free me up for the StormCloud and BrightLoop recovery items, I'd rather you know now.'
6 A message was sent to at least one account manager, Mina Hashimoto ([mina.hashimoto@moveops.com](mailto:mina.hashimoto@moveops.com), AM for BrightLoop, Vectral, StormCloud) or Emeka Diallo ([emeka.diallo@moveops.com](mailto:emeka.diallo@moveops.com), AM for Mosaic Robotics), via email (\`send_email\`), Slack DM (\`conversations_add_message\`), or a Slack channel message that tags or names the AM.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓—✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent posted a message to #customer-engagement tagging both @mina.hashimoto and @emeka.diallo by name with client-specific items. This satisfies the criterion of looping in account managers via a Slack channel message that names the AMs.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows two `conversations_add_message` calls to #customer-engagement: one tagging @mina.hashimoto covering StormCloud/BrightLoop/Vectral items, and one tagging @emeka.diallo covering Mosaic items.
✓Run #3Thread replies were posted to the #operations channel tagging @mina.hashimoto with StormCloud/Vectral/BrightLoop specifics and @emeka.diallo with Mosaic specifics. These are Slack channel messages that name/tag the AMs directly.
✓Run #4Two `conversations_add_message` calls to channel C002 tag @mina.hashimoto (covering Vectral and BrightLoop issues) and @emeka.diallo (covering Mosaic issues). Both account managers were directly messaged.
—Run #5No justification
✓Run #6The trajectory shows a `conversations_add_message` call to #customer-engagement that tags @mina.hashimoto and @emeka.diallo, reaching both account managers with client-facing issue details.
7 The message to the account manager(s), whether via email or Slack, identifies at least one client-facing issue requiring follow-up, such as the Jae-won Kim unsigned lease (StormCloud), Simone Richter apartment mismatch (BrightLoop), Marcus Webb vehicle delay (BrightLoop), or Deshawn Hartley move status (Mosaic).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓—✓ 100%
✓Run #1The #customer-engagement message to Mina explicitly identifies the Jae-won Kim unsigned lease (StormCloud), the Marcus Webb vehicle shipping delay (BrightLoop), and the Camille Oduya stipend gap (Vectral). The message to Emeka covers Mosaic status. Multiple actionable client-facing issues are identified.
✓Run #2The Mina message explicitly identifies: Jae-won Kim's unsigned lease (guarantor docs never sent), Simone Richter's studio vs 1BR mismatch, Marcus Webb's vehicle delay, and the Vectral batch status. The Emeka message identifies Deshawn Hartley's trade show deadline status and Andre Mitchell's move. Both messages are actionable and client-specific.
✓Run #3The thread message to @mina.hashimoto explicitly details the Jae-won Kim unsigned lease (StormCloud), Simone Richter studio vs. 1BR (BrightLoop), Marcus Webb completion unverified (BrightLoop), and Vectral ADA housing issues — all client-facing issues requiring follow-up.
✓Run #4The message to Mina Hashimoto identifies three specific client-facing issues: Terrence Nakamura's ADA housing and split shipment (Vectral), Camille Oduya's unverified stipend (Vectral), and Marcus Webb's vehicle shipping approval (BrightLoop). The message to Emeka covers Deshawn Hartley and Andre Mitchell (Mosaic).
—Run #5No justification
✓Run #6The #customer-engagement message to the AMs explicitly details: Jae-won Kim unsigned lease (StormCloud), Terrence Nakamura ADA housing gaps (Vectral), Camille Oduya stipend misconfiguration (Vectral), Simone Richter studio vs 1BR (BrightLoop), and Marcus Webb vehicle delay (BrightLoop).
8 The final response or write actions identify that Jae-won Kim's (StormCloud Analytics) Austin lease at Barton Creek Flats remains unsigned because the corporate guarantor packet was never sent to UrbanNest or the landlord.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓—— 100%
✓Run #1The agent clearly identifies in both the final response and the Airtable/Linear updates that Jae-won Kim's Austin lease at Barton Creek Flats was never finalized because the corporate guarantor packet requested by Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest was never sent, with Jae-won's temp housing expiring April 12.
✓Run #2The final response explicitly states: 'Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest emailed you on April 8 asking for MoveOps corporate guarantor docs... You replied promising to send them, but they were never sent.' The #operations and Mina Slack messages also detail this. The loop was clearly identified as not closed.
